data.description: The octahedron photo arrangement has the correct photo ORDER, but some photos are rotated incorrectly. Rule: the top of every photo should point AWAY from the octahedron's equator. (1) Photos on the upper half of the octahedron: top of photo should point up (away from center). (2) Photos on the lower half: top of photo should point down (away from center, toward the bottom point). Scope: (1) locate the octahedron component (likely src/components/* with three.js or CSS 3D — note the THREE.WebGLRenderer reference in the user's console log suggests three.js); (2) review each face's photo and its current rotation; (3) flip/rotate any photos that don't match the rule so the top of the photo points outward from the equator. Do NOT change the photo ORDER, only orientation. Acceptance: every photo on the octahedron has its 'top edge' pointing away from the equator — top-half photos read right-side-up from above, bottom-half photos read right-side-up from below.
data.title: Octahedron photo orientation: top photos face up, bottom photos face down

data.body: Review pass. Verified: (1) only src/components/three/OctahedronHero.tsx changed (+27/-11 in one commit 74d1e86); (2) UV loop now detects pole vertex via ys.findIndex(y => Math.abs(y) > 0.5) and assigns it (0.5, 1.0); (3) the two equator vertices get (0,0) and (1,0) deterministically via [0,1,2].filter(i => i !== poleIdx) so lower index gets (0,0); (4) FACE_SECTION_MAP and photo paths untouched (grep confirms); (5) pnpm typecheck clean. Minor note: findIndex returns -1 if no vertex matches |y|>0.5, which would silently leave uvForTri[-1] write as a no-op and produce a degenerate UV triangle — but octahedron geometry guarantees one pole vertex per face so this is theoretical. Acceptance criteria from plan all met at the code level. Visual right-side-up verification deferred to human per scope. No implementation-level rework needed.
